title: Tamil Tomato Thokku

subs.name: 400 g canned crushed tomatoes — reduce cook time to 15 minutes

ingredients.name: gingelly oil (nallennai)
visual_checks: gingelly oil is integral to the thokku's flavour
ingredients.notes: gingelly oil is integral to the thokku's flavour

subs.name: coconut oil — the flavour is different but works well

steps.text: Heat gingelly oil in a heavy-bottomed kadai over medium flame. Splutter mustard seeds, then add dried red chillies and curry leaves and fry for 20 seconds. Add the shallots and cook until they turn deeply golden, about 6 minutes — don't rush this step.
Add the garlic and ginger and fry until the raw smell disappears, about 2 minutes. Keep the flame medium — garlic burns fast and will turn bitter.
Add the chopped tomatoes and stir to combine. Add red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric, and salt. Stir well and raise the flame to medium-high to get the tomatoes cooking quickly.

Cook on medium to medium-high heat, stirring every few minutes, for 20–25 minutes until the tomatoes completely break down, the water evaporates, and the mixture turns thick and glossy. The oil will start to separate around the edges — this is the sign the thokku is ready.

Check salt and remove from heat. The thokku should be a deep brick-red, intensely flavoured, and thick enough to mound on a spoon. Cool completely before transferring to a glass jar. Keeps refrigerated for up to a week.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: shallot → 1 large red onion, finely sliced; gingelly-oil → coconut oil — the flavour is different but works well; tomato → 400 g canned crushed tomatoes — reduce cook time to 15 minutes
